question: Will this hitch fit with my factory bumper or do I need to modify it?

answers.answer: No modification needed. It bolts directly to the frame behind the bumper. The whole install took me about 45 minutes.
Factory bumper stays on. You may need to trim the plastic bumper fascia slightly on some models to expose the receiver opening, but the hitch itself is a direct bolt-on.

question: What is the tongue weight capacity on this hitch?

answers.answer: The Class III rating gives you 500 lbs tongue weight and 3,500 lbs gross trailer weight. Make sure you also check your vehicle towing capacity as that can be the limiting factor.

question: Does this come with the pin and clip or do I need to buy that separately?

answers.answer: It comes with a standard 5/8 inch hitch pin and clip. Works fine for most accessories. If you want a locking pin you will need to buy that separately.

question: How does this compare to the CURT Class III? Is the Max-Frame worth the extra cost?

answers.answer: The Max-Frame uses a heavier gauge steel and has a beefier weld pattern. If you are towing near the max capacity regularly it is worth it. For lighter loads either one works great.

question: Is the receiver opening a standard 2 inch?

answers.answer: Yes, standard 2 x 2 inch receiver. Compatible with all standard 2 inch ball mounts, bike racks, cargo carriers, etc.

question: Can I still use my truck bed normally with the Turnoverball installed?

answers.answer: That is the whole point of the Turnoverball design. The ball flips over and sits flush with the bed floor when not in use. You get full bed access. I have had mine for 2 years and love it.
Yes. When flipped down the ball is completely hidden. The cover plate sits flush. I can load full sheets of plywood no problem.

question: Does the Class IV rating mean I can tow a full-size travel trailer?

answers.answer: The hitch is rated for 10,000 lbs gross trailer weight, but your vehicle also needs to be rated for that. Check your door jamb sticker for your GCWR. The hitch is only one part of the equation.
I pull a 7500 lb travel trailer with mine no issues. Like the team said make sure your truck can handle it too. Also invest in a good weight distribution hitch for anything over 5000 lbs.

question: What can you actually use a front mount hitch for?

answers.answer: Front mount hitches are great for snow plows, winch mounts, front-mounted cargo carriers, and making it way easier to hook up a trailer. I use mine for a snow plow in winter and a bike rack when camping.
I use mine with a front-mounted receiver winch for off-road recovery. Also handy for hooking up boat trailers at the ramp since you can see the coupler.

question: Is the EcoHitch really completely hidden when installed?

answers.answer: The receiver hides behind the bumper fascia. You cannot see it at all when there is no accessory mounted. Only the receiver opening is visible and it sits pretty close to the bottom. Very clean look.

question: What makes this a commercial grade hitch vs a regular Class V?

answers.answer: Heavier wall tubing, dual-stage coating process (e-coat plus powder coat), and it meets the J684 commercial standard. The welds are robotically done for consistency. We use these across our fleet of 40 trucks.

question: Does the BumpStep actually protect the bumper from being backed into things?

answers.answer: It extends past the bumper by about 4 inches so yes it takes the hit instead of your bumper. The rubber step pad absorbs impact. Saved my tailgate twice in parking lots already.

question: Does this wiring harness provide a powered converter or does it tap into the vehicle wiring directly?

answers.answer: The T-connector uses a powered converter module. It does not splice into your vehicle wires. Plugs into the factory taillight connectors and provides a clean signal to the trailer.

question: Will this work with LED trailer lights or only incandescent?

answers.answer: Works with both. The powered converter handles the load difference automatically. No additional resistors needed for LED trailers.

question: Is the 7-way connector weatherproof? My truck sits outside year round.

answers.answer: It comes with a weatherproof cap for when the trailer is not connected. The connector itself is sealed. I have had mine exposed to rain and snow for 3 winters with no corrosion.

question: Is this compatible with a weight distribution system?

answers.answer: Yes, the Class III receiver works with standard weight distribution hitches. Just make sure whatever WD system you get is rated within the hitch capacity.

question: Is the Sportframe hitch strong enough for a small utility trailer?

answers.answer: Absolutely. The Sportframe is a Class I/II depending on application, rated for 2000-3500 lbs GTW. Perfect for small utility trailers, bike racks, and cargo carriers.

question: Will this work on my 2019 Wrangler JL or only the older JK?

answers.answer: The JH46 is specifically designed for the JK (2007-2018). For the JL you need the JH45 model. Make sure to check fitment for your exact year and model.

question: How heavy is this hitch? Can two people install it or do I need a lift?

answers.answer: The Ranch Hand Legend is pretty beefy - around 90-100 lbs depending on the application. Two strong people can manage it but having a floor jack to hold it in position while you bolt it up makes life a lot easier.

question: Is this a direct plug-and-play for a 2020 F-150 or do I need to splice any wires?

answers.answer: It is fully plug-and-play for the 2020 F-150. No splicing needed - it connects directly to the factory connector behind the tail light.

question: Will this work with LED tail lights or only halogen?

answers.answer: This harness works with both LED and halogen tail lights. It has built-in load resistors so you will not get hyperflash with LEDs.

question: Does this come with the 4-way flat connector or do I need to buy it separately?

answers.answer: Yes, the 4-way flat connector is included in the kit along with all mounting hardware.

question: How long is the wire lead from the connector to the vehicle harness?

answers.answer: The lead is approximately 56 inches, which is plenty of length for most installations. You can always extend it if needed.

question: Is this compatible with a 2019 Silverado 1500?

answers.answer: Yes, Hopkins makes a specific version for the 2019+ Silverado 1500. Just make sure you select the correct vehicle when ordering.

question: Can I use this to connect a small utility trailer with just running lights?

answers.answer: Absolutely. A 4-way flat is the standard connector for small trailers with tail, turn, and brake lights. Perfect for utility trailers.

question: Is the connector weatherproof?

answers.answer: It has a dust cover but is not fully submersible. I would recommend adding a connector bracket to keep it off the ground and protected.

question: Does this module handle the power conversion for European-style trailers?

answers.answer: The Modulite HD Plus handles US-standard trailer wiring. For European trailers you would need an additional adapter to convert from the 13-pin round to the US standard.

question: Will this work with a heavy-duty 7-way connector for my RV?

answers.answer: Yes, the HD Plus is rated for heavy-duty applications and works great with 7-way connections for RVs. It has built-in circuit protection too.

question: Is this harness long enough to reach from the bed-mounted connector to the cab?

answers.answer: Yes, it is designed specifically for 5th wheel setups. The harness is about 8 feet long which covers most full-size truck beds.

question: What makes this different from a standard T-connector harness?

answers.answer: The ZCI uses the vehicle data bus to control trailer lights instead of tapping into tail light wires. This means zero contact with factory wiring, which is important for vehicles with sensitive electronic systems.

question: Will this trigger any dashboard warning lights?

answers.answer: No, since it works through the data bus properly, it will not trigger any warning lights. That is one of the big advantages of the ZCI system.

question: My truck has separate turn and brake signals - will this converter handle that?

answers.answer: Yes, that is exactly what this converter is designed for. It combines the separate brake and turn signals into a single circuit for standard trailer lights.

question: Is the center pin for the 12V accessory circuit or the reverse light?

answers.answer: The center pin is for the 12V auxiliary power circuit. The reverse light circuit is on pin 2. This follows the standard SAE J2863 configuration.

question: Does this come pre-wired or do I need to wire each pin myself?

answers.answer: It comes with about 4 feet of pre-wired leads color-coded to industry standards. You will just need to connect them to your vehicle wiring.

question: How many inches of rise and drop does this ball mount offer?

answers.answer: The CURT Adjustable offers up to 6 inches of drop and 5.25 inches of rise, so it is very versatile for leveling your trailer.

question: Can I switch between a 2-inch and 2-5/16 ball without tools?

answers.answer: You will need a wrench to swap the hitch ball, but the adjustable height can be changed without tools using the pin and clip system.

question: What is the tongue weight capacity on this?

answers.answer: The tongue weight capacity is 1,200 lbs with a gross towing weight of 12,000 lbs. Make sure that matches your receiver class.

question: Can this be stored in the flipped-up position while driving without a trailer?

answers.answer: Yes! That is one of the best features of the Tow and Stow. You flip it up and it sits tight against the bumper so you barely notice it. No need to remove it.

question: Does the dual-ball version rattle at all?

answers.answer: Mine has a slight rattle when empty but B&W sells a silencer pad that eliminates it completely. Very common upgrade.

question: Is this compatible with the Blue Ox Avail tow bar?

answers.answer: Yes, Blue Ox designs their ball mounts to work seamlessly with all their tow bars including the Avail. It is actually the recommended ball mount for that setup.

question: What size pintle hook fits this mount?

answers.answer: This mount accepts standard pintle hooks with a 2-inch shank. CURT makes matching pintle hooks that bolt right on.

question: Can I use a combination pintle hook and ball with this?

answers.answer: Absolutely. A combo pintle/ball hook is a great choice for versatility. Just make sure the combined weight rating matches your towing needs.

question: How does the sway control on this work compared to a separate sway bar?

answers.answer: The Strait-Line uses a trunnion-style spring bar design that provides integrated sway control. It is generally considered more effective than bolt-on sway bars because it is always active.

question: Does the built-in scale actually work well for measuring tongue weight?

answers.answer: It is surprisingly accurate - within about 50 lbs in my experience. Not as precise as a dedicated tongue weight scale, but incredibly convenient for quick checks before every trip.

question: What is the maximum tongue weight this can measure?

answers.answer: The E-Series can measure up to 1,600 lbs of tongue weight. The gauge is right on the shank so you can read it easily.

question: What shank diameter does this ball have?

answers.answer: The 2-5/16 inch ball comes with a 1-1/4 inch shank diameter and is 2-5/8 inches long. Fits standard ball mount holes.

question: How accurate is the built-in tongue weight scale?

answers.answer: The Weigh Safe scale is certified accurate to within plus or minus 100 lbs. For a built-in scale that is quite good. We always recommend it for anyone serious about safe towing.

question: Does the aluminum construction hold up to salt and winter conditions?

answers.answer: The stainless steel and aluminum construction is actually better in salt and winter conditions than painted steel ball mounts. No rust at all after 3 winters for me.

question: Do I still need a separate sway control with this system?

answers.answer: No, the Continuum has built-in sway control through its unique head design. It actively resists sway without needing friction bars or any additional equipment.

question: Is this difficult to set up for the first time?

answers.answer: The initial setup takes about 30-45 minutes to get the spring bars tensioned correctly. After that, hookup and unhookup is quick. B&W has great setup videos on YouTube.

question: Can I see the braking output on the display while driving?

answers.answer: Yes, the P3 has a large 3-axis inertia display that shows real-time braking force. It is one of the best displays in any brake controller.

question: Does the Prodigy P3 work with electric-over-hydraulic brakes?

answers.answer: No, the P3 is designed for electric brakes only. For electric-over-hydraulic brakes you would need the Tekonsha Prodigy P3 EOH model which is a different unit.

question: How many axles can this controller handle?

answers.answer: The P3 can handle up to 4 trailer braking axles. It is rated for trailers with up to 8 brakes total.

question: Is this proportional or time-delayed?

answers.answer: The TriFlex is a proportional brake controller with triple-axis inertia sensing. It applies trailer brakes proportionally to how hard you brake in the tow vehicle.

question: Does it come with a mounting bracket?

answers.answer: Yes, CURT includes a snap-in dash mount bracket. It also comes with a wiring harness for a clean installation.

question: Is the Voyager good enough for occasional towing or should I get the P3?

answers.answer: The Voyager is a solid proportional controller that is great for occasional towing. The P3 adds features like the digital display and boost function, but the Voyager provides excellent braking performance at a lower price point.

question: Is this a time-delayed controller?

answers.answer: Yes, the Brakeman IV is a time-delayed controller. It is simple and reliable but does not modulate braking force based on deceleration like proportional controllers do.

question: Can I adjust the braking intensity on the fly?

answers.answer: Yes, there is a manual slide control for adjusting the maximum braking output, and you can use the manual override lever anytime while driving.

question: How does a phone-based brake controller work? Is it safe?

answers.answer: The Echo uses your phone accelerometer for proportional braking while the actual control module mounts at the trailer connector. It has been thoroughly tested and is DOT-compliant. Your phone just acts as the display and adjustment interface.

question: What happens if my phone dies while towing?

answers.answer: The Echo module continues to work with the last settings even if your phone disconnects. You just will not be able to adjust settings until you reconnect. The braking function itself is completely independent.

question: Does this actually help with trailer sway or is it just a brake controller?

answers.answer: It is primarily a brake controller but has a sway detection feature that can selectively apply trailer brakes to counteract sway. Not a replacement for proper weight distribution but it adds a safety layer.

question: What is the difference between the P2 and the Voyager?

answers.answer: The P2 has a digital display and uses bi-axis accelerometer sensing versus the Voyager pendulum-based system. The P2 also has a boost function for aggressive braking situations. Overall it slots between the Voyager and P3.

question: Does orientation matter when mounting the P2?

answers.answer: No, the P2 can be mounted at any angle or orientation. It has automatic leveling calibration so it adjusts itself regardless of mounting position.

question: Can I control this from my steering wheel buttons?

answers.answer: The I-Command does not integrate with steering wheel controls directly, but it does have a wireless remote you can mount on the dash or steering column for easy access.

question: Is this easy to install as a DIY project?

answers.answer: Very easy. The Venturer plugs into the factory brake controller port under the dash on most trucks. If your truck has the port, it is literally a 5-minute install with no tools.

question: How do I know if my truck has the factory brake controller port?

answers.answer: Check under the dash on the driver side near the steering column. You will see an unused connector - usually a 2-pin or 4-pin plug. Most full-size trucks from 2015 and newer have it if they have the tow package.

question: How wide is the step pad on these?

answers.answer: The Podium LG has a full 7-inch wide step pad that runs the entire length. Very generous stepping area.

question: Do these require drilling into the frame?

answers.answer: No drilling required. They use vehicle-specific brackets that bolt to existing factory mounting points under the cab.

question: How much lower do these hang compared to standard running boards?

answers.answer: The HDX Drop Steps hang about 3 inches lower than typical running boards. This makes a huge difference for lifted trucks - much easier to get in.

question: Will these hold up in winter with road salt?

answers.answer: Absolutely. The HDX has a textured black powder coat finish over steel construction. I have had mine for two winters in Michigan with zero rust issues.

question: Can I use these on my crew cab and still access the rear doors easily?

answers.answer: Yes, they are designed for crew cab applications. The step area extends under all four doors.

question: How reliable is the electric motor? What if it fails?

answers.answer: The motors are very reliable with a rated lifespan of over 10 years. If one fails, the step defaults to the retracted position so it will not drag on the ground. AMP offers a 5-year warranty on the motors.

question: Do these deploy automatically when I open the door?

answers.answer: Yes, the PowerSteps deploy automatically when any door is opened and retract when all doors are closed. It is seamless and takes about 1 second.

question: Will these affect my ground clearance when off-roading?

answers.answer: When retracted they tuck up tight under the rocker panel, so they do not reduce your ground clearance at all. That is the main advantage over fixed running boards for off-road use.

question: What is the weight capacity per step?

answers.answer: The HD2 running boards are rated at 500 lbs per step. They are heavy-duty steel construction built to handle anything.

question: Do these come in any color besides black?

answers.answer: They only come in the textured black finish from Rough Country. The finish is durable and matches most aftermarket accessories well.

question: Are these running boards compatible with a body lift?

answers.answer: Yes, but you may need the optional drop bracket kit to compensate for the extra height from the body lift. Go Rhino sells them separately.

question: How wide is the stepping surface?

answers.answer: The RB20 has a 5-inch wide step area with an integrated grip strip. Not as wide as some options but the grip texture makes it feel very secure.

question: Are these the round tube style?

answers.answer: Yes, the R7 is a 7-inch diameter round tube nerf bar with a stepped pad area. It gives that classic nerf bar look with more stepping surface than traditional 3-inch tubes.

question: Are these aluminum or steel?

answers.answer: The Summit Ridge boards are extruded aluminum with a black anodized finish. They are significantly lighter than steel boards which is nice for fuel economy.

question: Do they come with all the mounting hardware included?

answers.answer: Yes, vehicle-specific mounting brackets and all hardware are included. The brackets are steel for strength even though the boards themselves are aluminum.

question: How does the star texture grip compare to traditional rubber pads?

answers.answer: The star armor texture is all metal with raised grip points. It works better in mud and snow than rubber pads because debris does not stick to it. Rubber pads can get slippery when wet.

question: Does this fold up flush when not in use?

answers.answer: Yes, the BedStep2 folds up against the side of the bed and is barely noticeable. You just flip it down when you need to access the bed.

question: Can I install this with a tonneau cover?

answers.answer: Yes, it mounts to the side of the bed near the tailgate and does not interfere with any tonneau cover style - roll-up, tri-fold, or retractable.

question: What material are these made from?

answers.answer: The Pro Series is made from heavy gauge steel with a durable black powder coat finish. They include stainless steel trim accents for a premium look.

question: Will the installer need to drill any holes?

answers.answer: No drilling on most applications. They use factory mounting points with vehicle-specific brackets. Installation takes about 30-45 minutes with basic hand tools.

question: Do these require painting or do they come in a finished color?

answers.answer: They come in a matte black textured finish that looks great as-is on most trucks. They can be painted to match your truck color, but most people run them in the factory matte black.

question: How much additional tire coverage do these provide?

answers.answer: The Pocket Style flares add about 1.5 inches of coverage per side. That is usually enough to cover 33-inch tires on a leveled truck without rubbing issues.

question: Are the pocket bolt accents real or decorative?

answers.answer: They are decorative stainless steel bolts - the flares actually attach using hardware behind the fender. The pocket bolts give it that custom wide-body look.

question: How far do these extend beyond the stock fender?

answers.answer: The Extend-A-Fender adds approximately 2.5 inches beyond the stock fender line. They provide the most coverage of any Bushwacker flare style.

question: Will these fit over my factory mud flaps?

answers.answer: You will likely need to remove or trim the factory mud flaps for a proper fit. The flares extend further out than where the mud flaps mount.

question: Are these OEM quality or aftermarket grade?

answers.answer: EGR is actually an OEM supplier to several manufacturers. These flares are made with the same ABS material and processes as factory flares. Fit and finish is top-notch.

question: Do they come with a rubber gasket seal?

answers.answer: Yes, they include a foam rubber seal gasket that prevents moisture and debris from getting between the flare and the body. Really helps prevent paint damage.

question: Are these the same as Bushwacker Pocket Style?

answers.answer: They have a similar look but are a different product. The Rough Country flares are more budget-friendly and use PP plastic versus the Bushwacker ABS. Both look good but the Bushwacker has a slightly more refined finish.

question: Are these designed specifically for Jeep Wranglers?

answers.answer: Yes, the M1 flares are purpose-built for Jeep JK and JL Wranglers. They are steel construction with a bolt-on design that is much more durable than plastic flares for serious off-road use.

question: Will I lose my inner fender liner with these?

answers.answer: The M1 flares do not include an inner liner, but you can reuse your factory liners in most cases by trimming them slightly. Some people run without them for better mud clearing.

question: Can these be color matched by a body shop?

answers.answer: Yes, the smooth ABS surface takes paint very well. Any body shop can prep and paint them to match your vehicle color. Just make sure they use a flexible paint additive.

question: Are these the same flares that come on the factory widebody package?

answers.answer: Yes, these are genuine Mopar parts identical to what comes on the factory widebody option. Perfect OEM fit and finish with the Mopar logo embossed.

question: Do I need to transfer any clips from my old flares?

answers.answer: No, the Mopar kit includes all new clips, fasteners, and push pins. Everything you need is in the box.

question: What is the difference between Forge style and Pocket style?

answers.answer: The Forge style has a smooth, flush mount design without the decorative pocket bolts. It gives a more modern, factory-integrated look compared to the Pocket style rugged appearance.

question: Are these no-drill or do I need to make holes?

answers.answer: The Ruff Riderz use a combination of factory holes and 3M automotive tape for attachment. No drilling into the body is needed.

question: How durable is the tape attachment in extreme heat?

answers.answer: The 3M tape is automotive-grade and rated for extreme temperatures. I live in Arizona and mine have been solid for over two years with no lifting or peeling.

question: How do these attach without drilling?

answers.answer: They use existing hardware and factory holes behind your wheels. The mounting brackets clip into existing body panel fastener locations. Truly no drilling required.

question: Will these clear my aftermarket 20-inch wheels?

answers.answer: They should clear most 20-inch wheel setups. The flaps mount behind the wheel well and have a contoured shape that avoids contact. If you have very aggressive offset wheels, check your clearance first.

question: Do these fit behind factory wheel well liners?

answers.answer: Yes, Husky designs these to work with the factory wheel well liners. They mount between the liner and the body panel using existing fastener locations.

question: Are these flexible or rigid?

answers.answer: They are made from a thermoplastic material that is semi-rigid. Stiff enough to deflect debris but flexible enough to not crack from impacts or in cold weather.

question: Are these specifically designed for off-road trucks?

answers.answer: Yes, the Trail Armor line is designed for lifted and off-road trucks. They hang lower and wider than standard mud flaps to compensate for larger tires and increased wheel travel.

question: Can I get these with a custom logo?

answers.answer: Yes! Gatorback offers custom logo options including American flag, No Limit, and various manufacturer logos. They also do fully custom designs if you contact them directly.

question: How thick is the rubber on these?

answers.answer: The Gatorback flaps are 3/8 inch thick recycled rubber. Very heavy duty and will not crack or fade. They also have a lifetime warranty.

question: What makes these different from regular mud flaps?

answers.answer: Mud Skins are much thinner and more form-fitting than traditional mud flaps. They contour to the wheel well shape for a cleaner look while still providing splash protection.

question: Do these work with the Rough Country lift kit brackets?

answers.answer: Yes, they are designed to work with Rough Country lift kits and the existing mounting points. The added length accounts for the extra height from the lift.

question: Will these drag on the ground with a stock height truck?

answers.answer: They are a bit long for stock height trucks honestly. They are optimized for trucks with a 2-4 inch lift. On a stock truck you might want to trim them or go with a different option.

question: Are these designed for rally cars or can I use them on a daily driver?

answers.answer: They are rally-inspired but work great on daily drivers, especially Subarus, Focuses, and other sport compacts. The polyurethane material is extremely durable for everyday use.

question: How do these handle highway speeds without flapping?

answers.answer: The textured polypropylene material is rigid enough that it does not flap at highway speeds. The contoured mounting keeps them tight to the body.

question: Can I install these myself or do I need a shop?

answers.answer: Definitely a DIY-friendly install. Just basic hand tools and about 20 minutes per side. The instructions are clear and vehicle-specific brackets are included.

question: Are these the same as the dealer-installed splash guards?

answers.answer: Yes, these are the exact same Mopar genuine accessories that dealers install. You are just saving the dealer markup and labor by installing them yourself.

question: Will this affect my front-facing sensors or camera?

answers.answer: The Legend grille guard is designed with provisions for factory sensors and cameras on most applications. However, some adaptive cruise control radar sensors may need to be relocated. Check your specific vehicle application notes.

question: How much does this weigh? Will it affect my suspension?

answers.answer: The Legend weighs approximately 85-100 lbs depending on vehicle application. It should not noticeably affect your suspension but you may want to consider a leveling kit if your truck is stock height.

question: Can I add auxiliary lights to this grille guard?

answers.answer: Absolutely. The Legend has multiple light mounting tabs welded in. It is pre-drilled for most popular light sizes and brands.

question: Is the HDX modular or one piece?

answers.answer: The HDX is a modular design with a separate uprights and cross bars. This makes shipping easier and allows for some customization in the final look.

question: Will this void my bumper warranty?

answers.answer: Aftermarket grille guards generally do not void your vehicle warranty under the Magnuson-Moss Act. However, if the guard directly causes damage to the bumper, that specific damage would not be covered.

question: Does the step plate actually help for accessing the engine bay?

answers.answer: Yes, the integrated step is rated for 300 lbs and makes it much easier to reach the windshield or check fluids on taller trucks. Very practical feature.

question: Is this compatible with front parking sensors?

answers.answer: Most Go Rhino applications include sensor relocation brackets or cutouts. Check the vehicle-specific application guide to confirm for your exact truck.

question: Does this replace the factory bumper completely?

answers.answer: Yes, it is a full bumper replacement that bolts to the factory frame mounts. You remove the entire factory bumper and fascia.

question: Will my airbag sensors still work with this replacement bumper?

answers.answer: The N-Fab guard maintains the factory crash structure mounting points so your airbag sensors remain functional. Always verify with a scan after install to make sure no codes are thrown.

question: What is the tubing diameter on this guard?

answers.answer: The Pro Series uses 1.5-inch diameter steel tubing with a one-piece design. It provides solid protection without the bulk of larger 2-inch tube guards.

question: Is this heavy enough to handle a deer strike?

answers.answer: The Xtreme Guard is heavy-gauge steel and can deflect deer and other large animal impacts. It is one of the heavier-duty options available. Many ranchers and rural drivers swear by Frontier guards.

question: Does this interfere with the factory tow hooks?

answers.answer: The Frontier guard has provisions for factory tow hook access on most applications. Some models even relocate the hooks to the front face of the guard for easier access.

question: Is the LED light bar included or sold separately?

answers.answer: The LED light bar is included! It is a 20-inch light bar that mounts in the center of the bull bar. Wiring harness with a switch is also included.

question: Does this work with front license plate mounting?

answers.answer: Yes, Lund includes a license plate relocation bracket that mounts to the bull bar. Works great for states that require a front plate.

question: Can I powder coat this a different color?

answers.answer: Yes, the steel construction takes powder coat very well. Many customers have theirs done in body-matched colors. Just strip the factory finish first for best adhesion.

question: What is the difference between the Midnight and the Legend?

answers.answer: The Midnight has a sleeker, more low-profile design with a satin black finish. The Legend is the full grille guard style with more chrome and a taller profile. The Midnight is more about style while the Legend is maximum protection.

question: Does the Midnight bumper accept a winch?

answers.answer: Yes, the Midnight bumper has a built-in winch mount that accepts most standard winches up to 12,000 lbs. The winch is hidden behind the bumper face for a clean look.

question: Are these DOT approved for street use?

answers.answer: Yes, Morimoto XB headlights are fully DOT and SAE compliant. They have proper beam cutoff patterns and will pass inspection in all 50 states.

question: How does the light output compare to factory HID headlights?

answers.answer: The XB LEDs put out significantly more usable light than factory HIDs. The beam pattern is wider and more uniform. Night and day difference, pun intended.

question: Do the DRLs have a sequential turn signal?

answers.answer: Yes, the XB headlights feature sequential LED turn signals that sweep from inside to outside. They also have a switchback DRL that changes from white to amber when signaling.

question: Can I change the DRL color on these?

answers.answer: The NOVA Series comes with a choice of Alpha-Black, Chrome, or Jet-Black housing. The DRL itself is white/amber switchback but the housing color dramatically changes the overall look.

question: Will these cause any CAN bus errors on my newer truck?

answers.answer: AlphaRex includes anti-flicker modules with their headlights for vehicles that need them. Most newer trucks work plug-and-play with no errors.

question: Do I need to use HID bulbs with the projector housing or can I use LED?

answers.answer: The PRO-YD projectors accept standard bulbs. You can use halogen, HID, or LED bulbs. LED bulbs work great in the projector housing and give you instant-on capability.

question: Is the LED module replaceable if it burns out?

answers.answer: On the Anzo LED projector headlights the LED module is integrated and not separately replaceable. However, LEDs are rated for 50,000+ hours so they should outlast the vehicle.

question: Do these have built-in LED bulbs or do I buy them separate?

answers.answer: The Anzo LED projectors have integrated LED chips for both low and high beam. No separate bulbs to buy - everything is self-contained in the housing.

question: Are the halos RGB or just white?

answers.answer: RECON offers both white-only and RGBW halo options depending on the model. The RGBW version is app-controlled and can display any color. Check which version you are ordering.

question: Is this a complete headlight assembly or just replacement bulbs?

answers.answer: This is a replacement bulb kit, not a full headlight assembly. You get a pair of LED bulbs that go into your existing factory headlight housings. Very easy swap.

question: Will these cause dashboard warnings on my 2021 Ram?

answers.answer: Some 2021 Rams may throw a bulb-out warning with LED replacements. Rough Country includes anti-flicker decoders in the kit to prevent this.

question: How do these compare to the Rough Country LED bulbs?

answers.answer: The Morimoto 2Stroke 3.0 is a premium LED bulb with better color temperature consistency, higher lumen output, and a more precise beam pattern. They cost more but the light quality is noticeably better.

question: Does this convert my fog light housing into an extra headlight?

answers.answer: Essentially yes. The SS3 pod replaces your factory fog light and puts out headlight-level brightness with a proper beam pattern. It is a massive upgrade in visibility.

question: Is this legal for road use?

answers.answer: The SAE fog beam pattern version is street legal. The driving and spot beam patterns are for off-road use only. Make sure to select the SAE fog version for street use.

question: Can I control these from my phone?

answers.answer: Yes, the ColorSHIFT 2.0 controller connects via Bluetooth to the ORACLE app on iOS and Android. You can change colors, patterns, and brightness all from your phone.

question: Do I need to open my headlight housings to install these?

answers.answer: Yes, the halos mount inside the headlight housing. You will need to bake open your headlights, install the halos, then reseal them. It is an intermediate-level project.

question: What color temperature are the LEDs?

answers.answer: The Putco LED conversion bulbs run at 6000K which is a crisp white light. Not blue-tinted like some cheaper LEDs.

question: Do these have a sequential turn signal?

answers.answer: Yes, the XB LED tail lights feature a sequential turn signal that sweeps outward. It is a very clean effect and gets a lot of compliments.

question: Will these work with my trailer brake wiring harness?

answers.answer: Yes, they are plug-and-play and work with all factory wiring including trailer harness connections. No modification needed.

question: Can I switch between red and smoked lens?

answers.answer: No, the lens color is fixed at purchase. AlphaRex offers both red/clear and smoked versions. Make sure you order the style you want.

question: Are the brake lights bright enough with the smoked lens?

answers.answer: Yes, the LEDs are bright enough to be clearly visible even through the smoked lens. They are DOT compliant in both versions.

question: Are these full LED or do they use halogen for some functions?

answers.answer: The Spyder LED tail lights are fully LED for all functions - brake, turn, reverse, and running lights. No halogen bulbs anywhere.

question: Do these fit without any modification to the truck?

answers.answer: Yes, they are direct bolt-on replacements that use the factory mounting points and plug directly into the factory wiring harness. A 15-minute swap.

question: How is the build quality compared to OEM tail lights?

answers.answer: Anzo has improved their quality significantly. The housing is well-sealed and the LED boards are solid. Not quite OEM Morimoto level but very good for the price.

question: What does OLED mean for tail lights?

answers.answer: OLED (Organic LED) panels produce a very uniform, smooth light without visible individual LED dots. It gives a premium European car look. The light distribution is exceptionally even.

question: Will these fit in my factory tail light housing?

answers.answer: Yes, these are direct replacement LED bulbs for your factory housings. They come in all standard bases - 1157, 7443, 3157, etc. Just select the right bulb number for your vehicle.

question: Do these cause hyperflash when used as turn signals?

answers.answer: They can on some vehicles. Putco includes a load resistor in the package to prevent hyperflash. Just wire it in parallel and you are good to go.

question: Are these designed for Mustangs only?

answers.answer: Raxiom makes the Axial Series for several vehicles but they are best known for their Mustang applications. Check availability for your specific vehicle.

question: Can I add the ColorSHIFT feature to these tail lights?

answers.answer: Some ORACLE tail light models have ColorSHIFT halos available as an add-on. However, the main brake and turn functions remain standard red and amber for DOT compliance.

question: Are these actual OE replacements or aftermarket style?

answers.answer: They are aftermarket lights designed to match the OE look closely. They are a great budget-friendly replacement if you have a cracked or faded factory tail light. Not genuine OEM parts though.

question: Do these come with bulbs?

answers.answer: Yes, all required bulbs are included in the box. They use standard halogen bulbs which are easy to find if you ever need replacements.

question: How many lumens does this 50-inch bar put out?

answers.answer: The E-Series 50-inch produces approximately 46,800 raw lumens. It is an absolute wall of light. Rigid uses the most efficient Osram LEDs in the industry.

question: Is this legal to run on public roads?

answers.answer: Light bars this size are for off-road use only. Running them on public roads is illegal in most states and will blind oncoming traffic. Use a cover when on-road.

question: What beam pattern does this come in?

answers.answer: The C-Series 40-inch comes in a combo beam pattern with spot in the center and flood on the outer sections. Gives you great distance and peripheral coverage.

question: Is KC still made in the USA?

answers.answer: KC HiLiTES designs everything in Williams, Arizona. Some components are sourced globally but final assembly and quality control is done in the US.

question: Does the curved shape affect the beam pattern compared to a straight bar?

answers.answer: The curve actually helps spread the beam pattern wider, giving you better peripheral illumination. You sacrifice a bit of distance compared to a straight bar but gain a wider field of view.

question: Does this come with a wiring harness?

answers.answer: Yes, Rough Country includes a complete wiring harness with relay, inline fuse, and a dash-mounted toggle switch. Everything you need for installation.

question: What justifies the premium price on Baja Designs?

answers.answer: Baja Designs uses the highest bin Cree LEDs, precision-machined reflectors, and a patented spot lens. The result is about 30-40 percent more usable light at distance compared to competitors. They also have a lifetime warranty.

question: Is this the same light bar used in Baja 1000 racing?

answers.answer: Yes, the OnX6+ is the exact model used by many Baja 1000 race teams. It is race-proven at high speeds in extreme conditions. That is the DNA behind the brand.

question: What is the backlight feature on the Radiance?

answers.answer: The Radiance has a broad-spectrum rear-facing LED backlight behind the optics. You can choose from multiple colors (red, blue, green, amber, or white) to customize your look. It gives the bar a unique glowing halo effect.

question: Can I run just the backlight without the main forward-facing LEDs?

answers.answer: Yes, the backlight and the main white forward LEDs are on separate circuits. You can run either one independently or both at the same time.

question: How does Nilight compare to premium brands like Rigid?

answers.answer: Nilight is a great value option. You get about 70% of the light output of premium brands at about 20% of the price. The build quality is decent for the money. Perfect for occasional off-road use.

question: Does this come with a wiring harness?

answers.answer: Yes, Nilight includes a complete wiring harness with a fuse, relay, and rocker switch. The harness quality is actually quite good for a budget brand.

question: Is Heretic Studio the same company that does the Jeep Gladiator lights?

answers.answer: Yes, Heretic Studio is a boutique lighting manufacturer based in Arizona. They are well known in the Jeep and UTV community for their quality and USA manufacturing.

question: Can I get different beam patterns for each pod?

answers.answer: Yes! Diode Dynamics sells each SS12 pod individually so you can mix and match. Common setup is spot for center and flood or driving for the outer pods.

question: Are these truly waterproof? I want to use them on my boat.

answers.answer: The SS12 is IP68 rated and fully submersible. Many customers use them in marine applications. The stainless steel hardware holds up to saltwater as well.

question: What is the halo feature on the XPR?

answers.answer: The XPR Halo has a ring of position lights around each LED optic. They serve as low-power DRLs and give the bar a distinctive look when the main beam is off.

question: Is 52 inches too big for a mid-size truck?

answers.answer: 52 inches is designed for full-size truck roofs. On a mid-size truck it would overhang the edges. I would recommend a 40 or 42 inch bar for mid-size applications.

question: Does the curved or straight version fit better on a roof mount?

answers.answer: Curved bars follow the roofline better and look more integrated. Straight bars work too but may require slightly different bracket angles. Most people prefer curved for roof mounting.

question: Do these produce a true fog light beam pattern or just a flood?

answers.answer: The Morimoto XB fogs produce a proper wide flat beam pattern with a sharp horizontal cutoff. This is exactly what you want in a fog light - wide coverage without blinding oncoming traffic.

question: Are these a direct replacement for factory fog lights?

answers.answer: Yes, Morimoto designs them as direct replacements. They use the factory mounting points and plug directly into your existing fog light connector. No modification needed.

question: What is the difference between Sport and Pro versions?

answers.answer: The Sport puts out about 1,600 lumens per pod while the Pro does 2,600 lumens. If you have good headlights already, the Sport is plenty. The Pro is for maximum fog visibility.

question: Can I get yellow fog lights instead of white?

answers.answer: Yes! Diode Dynamics offers the SS3 in both white and yellow. Many customers prefer yellow for fog lights as it reduces glare in fog, rain, and snow conditions.

question: Do these require any special wiring or can I use the factory fog light circuit?

answers.answer: They use the factory fog light circuit and connector. No additional wiring needed unless you want to wire them to a separate switch for independent control.

question: Are these the round LED replacements for the Jeep JK?

answers.answer: Yes, the Gravity G4 is designed as a direct replacement for the Jeep JK round fog light housing. They are one of the most popular fog light upgrades for JK Wranglers.

question: Can I use these as both fog lights and rock lights?

answers.answer: The D-Series Dually is versatile enough for both applications. For fog lights use the flood beam pattern, and for rock lights the diffused version works best. They are IP68 waterproof so mounting under the vehicle is no problem.

question: What mounting options are available?

answers.answer: The D-Series comes with a versatile L-bracket that allows surface, flush, or A-pillar mounting. Rigid also sells fog light specific brackets for direct OEM fog light replacement on many vehicles.

question: Is the Squadron Sport bright enough for trail riding at night?

answers.answer: The Squadron Sport puts out 3,150 lumens in the spot beam pattern. For trail riding you would want a pair which gives you excellent forward visibility. For high-speed desert riding, step up to the Squadron Pro.

question: Does this replace the entire fog light housing or just the bulb?

answers.answer: The Rough Country kit replaces the entire fog light assembly including the housing. It is a cleaner install than just swapping bulbs and the LED housing is designed for proper LED beam optics.

question: What is the warranty on these?

answers.answer: Putco offers a 3-year warranty on the Luminix LED fog lights. They also have a hassle-free replacement policy if you have any issues.

question: Are these brighter than factory fogs?

answers.answer: Significantly brighter. The Luminix LEDs put out about 3x the lumens of factory halogen fog bulbs with better color temperature and beam pattern.

question: Can these sync with my ORACLE ColorSHIFT headlight halos?

answers.answer: Yes, if you get the ColorSHIFT version of the fog lights they can be controlled from the same ORACLE app and controller as your headlight halos. Perfect color synchronization.

question: Are these meant to add fog lights where my truck did not come with them?

answers.answer: Yes, these are complete fog light kits for vehicles that did not come with factory fogs. They include the housings, bulbs, wiring harness, and switch. Great way to add fogs to a base model truck.

question: Will I need to cut my bumper to install these?

answers.answer: No cutting needed. Most vehicles have pre-scored fog light openings in the bumper even on base models. You just pop out the factory blanks and install the fog light housings.

question: Does BedRug absorb water and get moldy?

answers.answer: No, the BedRug material is 100% polypropylene which does not absorb water. Any water that gets on it drains through to the factory drain holes. It dries quickly and will not mold or mildew.

question: Can I still use tie-down anchors with this liner?

answers.answer: Yes, BedRug has cutouts for factory tie-down points. The carpet-like surface also helps prevent cargo from sliding around so you may not need to tie things down as often.

question: Is this comfortable to sit or kneel on in the bed?

answers.answer: That is actually one of the biggest selling points. It is like carpet padding and makes the truck bed usable as a workspace. Kneeling on it is way more comfortable than bare metal or spray-in liner.

question: How long does the LINE-X application take?

answers.answer: The actual spray application takes about 2-3 hours. You drop off your truck in the morning and pick it up in the afternoon. The liner is touch-dry in about an hour but full cure takes 24 hours.

question: Does LINE-X void the factory bed warranty?

answers.answer: LINE-X actually comes with its own lifetime warranty. The factory bed warranty is separate. Most dealers have no issue with professional spray-in liners.

question: Does this cover just the floor or the sides too?

answers.answer: The UltraGrip is a floor-only bed mat. It does not cover the sides, tailgate, or bulkhead. If you want full coverage, you would need a full bed liner instead.

question: What is the difference between over-rail and under-rail liners?

answers.answer: Over-rail liners fold over the top of the bed rails for maximum protection, while under-rail liners sit below the rail so you can still see the bed rail. Over-rail gives more protection but under-rail looks cleaner.

question: Will a tonneau cover still fit over an over-rail liner?

answers.answer: Some tonneau covers may not seal properly over an over-rail liner. If you plan to use a tonneau, I would recommend an under-rail liner instead. Check your tonneau cover manufacturer for compatibility.

question: How does the interlocking panel system work?

answers.answer: The DualLiner uses separate panels for each wall, the floor, and the tailgate. They interlock together and are custom-fit to your specific truck. The rubber floor mat sits over the ZeroSkid surface to prevent cargo movement.

question: How is the XLT different from the Classic BedRug?

answers.answer: The XLT is a mat-only product that covers just the floor and goes up the sides about 3 inches. The Classic is a full 5-piece system that covers the floor, sides, bulkhead, and tailgate. XLT is more affordable.

question: Is this thick enough to protect against dents?

answers.answer: The Rough Country mat is about 3/8 inch thick recycled rubber. It will protect against scratches and minor impacts but will not prevent dents from very heavy items being dropped in.

question: Does this have a strong rubber smell?

answers.answer: There is a mild rubber smell for the first week or two, especially in warm weather. It goes away fairly quickly though. Nothing overwhelming.

question: Can this be installed with a spray-in liner already in the bed?

answers.answer: Yes, you can layer the TechLiner over a spray-in liner. Some people do this to add a non-skid surface over a smooth spray-in liner. Works great as an added layer of protection.

question: Is Penda still in business? I had one on my old truck years ago.

answers.answer: Yes, Penda is still making bed liners. They have been around since the 1980s and their drop-in liners are still popular for their durability and value.

question: Can I still use my truck bed for hauling 4x8 sheets of plywood with this cover?

answers.answer: Yes! The MX4 folds all the way up against the cab window giving you full bed access. It also locks in several partially-open positions. Very versatile.

question: How waterproof is this cover?

answers.answer: The MX4 has integrated drain tubes and rubber seals at each panel joint. It keeps out about 95% of rain. In heavy sustained rain you might get a few drops through, but for normal weather it is essentially waterproof.

question: Does this work with a bed rack or bed-mounted accessories?

answers.answer: The MX4 is compatible with some low-profile bed racks but not all. Check BAK compatibility guides. It works fine with most bed-mounted tie-downs and toolboxes under 3 inches tall.

question: How secure is a roll-up cover compared to a hard folding cover?

answers.answer: A roll-up cover provides concealment but not hard security. Someone could cut through the vinyl with a knife. For valuable cargo, a hard folding or retractable cover is more secure. For everyday use, the TruXport is excellent.

question: How tight does this roll up at the cab? Can I still see out my rear window?

answers.answer: It rolls up into a fairly tight bundle about 6 inches in diameter. You can still see out the rear window but you will lose some visibility in the bottom portion. Most people get used to it quickly.

question: Can I lock the cover in a partially open position?

answers.answer: Yes, the M-Series locks at any point along the track. You can leave it 25%, 50%, or 75% open. The locking mechanism engages with a simple pull of the handle.

question: Where does the cover go when fully retracted?

answers.answer: It retracts into a canister at the front of the bed near the cab. The canister takes up about 10 inches of bed space but the remaining bed is completely clear and unobstructed.

question: Is this a soft or hard tri-fold?

answers.answer: The Trifecta 2.0 is a soft tri-fold with a fabric vinyl top. It is lightweight, easy to install, and very affordable. For a hard tri-fold, look at the Extang Solid Fold line.

question: What is the difference between the G2 and the MX4?

answers.answer: The G2 has a glossy FRP (fiberglass reinforced polymer) top panel while the MX4 has a matte aluminum top. The MX4 is more scratch resistant and has upgraded latches. The G2 is more budget-friendly.

question: Can I drive with the cover folded up on the highway?

answers.answer: Yes, the BAKFlip G2 is designed to be driven with the panels folded up against the rear window. It locks securely in the upright position. Many people drive like this daily.

question: Can I mount a utility rack on the RetraxPRO rails?

answers.answer: Yes! The RetraxPRO MX is designed to work with the Retrax Trax Rail system which accepts T-slot accessories including racks, crossbars, and tie-downs. It is one of the few retractable covers that supports a rack.

question: How durable is the aluminum cover material?

answers.answer: The RetraxPRO MX uses polycarbonate-coated aluminum slats. It can support up to 500 lbs of evenly distributed weight on top. Very strong and scratch resistant.

question: How does this compare to the Extang Trifecta 2.0?

answers.answer: Very similar in concept. The Rough Country is typically $50-100 less and has comparable quality. The Extang has slightly better vinyl material and clamp hardware, but for budget-conscious buyers the Rough Country is excellent.

question: Can I install this by myself?

answers.answer: Absolutely. It clamps onto the bed rails with no drilling. Takes about 15-20 minutes solo. Just make sure the bed rails are clean before you clamp down.

question: How thick are the hard panels?

answers.answer: The Flex panels are about 1.5 inches thick with a FRP composite top and EPS foam core. They are rigid enough to stand on - rated for 300 lbs of evenly distributed weight.

question: Does UnderCover offer a matte finish option?

answers.answer: The standard Flex comes in a textured black matte finish. There is no gloss or painted option, but the matte black looks great on virtually any truck color.

question: Is the retraction mechanism manual or powered?

answers.answer: The UltraGroove is manually operated. You release the latch and push the cover back. The mechanism is smooth and easy to operate with one hand. There is no motor to worry about failing.

question: Does the canister drain if water gets inside?

answers.answer: Yes, the Pace Edwards canister has built-in drain tubes that route water out through the bed drain holes. It handles rain and car wash water without any pooling.

question: Is the Access Original the same as the TonnoSport?

answers.answer: No, the Original is a step up from the TonnoSport. The Original has a stronger frame, better vinyl material, and a tighter fit. The TonnoSport is their budget entry-level option.

question: Can I really stand on this cover?

answers.answer: Yes, the DiamondBack HD is rated for 1,600 lbs of distributed load. You can literally stand on it, mount a roof top tent, or carry kayaks. It is the strongest tonneau cover on the market.

question: How much does this cover weigh?

answers.answer: The HD weighs between 180-220 lbs depending on bed size. It is a serious piece of equipment. Installation is a two-person job. The trade-off for that weight is incredible strength and security.

question: Does this lock securely enough to protect tools and equipment?

answers.answer: The DiamondBack uses a key-locking system and the cover itself is aluminum panels. It provides the best theft protection of any tonneau cover. Many contractors use them to secure expensive tools.

question: Do I need the specific Yakima towers or can I use existing factory rails?

answers.answer: The StreamLine crossbars require Yakima-specific towers or adapters for your vehicle. If you have factory side rails, you would use the RailBar towers. For bare roofs, you need the BaseLine towers.

question: How much wind noise do these bars create at highway speed?

answers.answer: The StreamLine bars have an aerodynamic profile that significantly reduces wind noise compared to square bars. You may hear a slight hum at 70+ mph but it is minimal. Adding a fairing eliminates it completely.

question: What is the weight capacity of the WingBar Evo?

answers.answer: The bars themselves can handle 165 lbs each but your vehicle roof rating is usually the limiting factor. Most SUVs and crossovers have a dynamic roof load rating of 150-165 lbs total.

question: Can I fit a Thule cargo box on these bars?

answers.answer: Yes, Thule designs all their cargo boxes to work with the WingBar Evo. The T-track on top of the bar accepts all Thule accessories including cargo boxes, bike racks, and kayak carriers.

question: How does Rhino-Rack compare to Thule and Yakima?

answers.answer: Rhino-Rack is comparable in quality and is often priced between the two. The Vortex bars are very quiet and strong. They are particularly popular in Australia where Rhino-Rack is based and heavily used for outback touring.

question: Is this compatible with a tonneau cover?

answers.answer: The OverHaul HD mounts to the bed rails above the tonneau cover, so it works with most covers. You do need at least 3/4 inch clearance between the cover and the crossbars. Check specific compatibility.

question: What is the weight capacity?

answers.answer: The OverHaul HD is rated for 500 lbs total capacity. That is enough for most cargo including lumber, kayaks, ladders, and even light roof top tents.

question: Can I mount a roof top tent on the Slimline II?

answers.answer: Yes, the Slimline II is one of the most popular platforms for roof top tents. It distributes the weight evenly across the mounting points. Frontrunner makes specific tent mounting brackets for it.

question: How much does this rack weigh?

answers.answer: The Slimline II weighs about 40-55 lbs depending on the size configuration. It is aluminum so it is lighter than comparable steel racks from other brands.

question: Is it possible to install a Frontrunner rack by myself?

answers.answer: The rack is manageable solo but much easier with two people. The trickiest part is lifting it onto the roof and aligning the mounting feet. Budget about 2-3 hours for installation.

question: Is the CapRock a full-length or half-length platform?

answers.answer: Thule offers the CapRock in multiple sizes. There are full-length options for SUVs and shorter versions for crossovers. Check the size chart for your specific vehicle.

question: Does the Prinsu rack block the sunroof?

answers.answer: Prinsu offers both full-length and cab-only versions. The cab-only version has a cutout for the sunroof so you can still open it. Check which version fits your needs.

question: What is the noise level at highway speeds?

answers.answer: The Prinsu rack does create some wind noise at highway speeds. Adding a wind deflector on the front edge helps a lot. The noise level is comparable to other full-length rack platforms.

question: Are these compatible with existing factory side rails?

answers.answer: Yes, CURT makes these crossbars in several widths to fit factory raised side rails. They use a universal clamp system that fits most rail profiles. Very affordable entry into roof-mounted cargo.

question: Can I mount a kayak carrier on these?

answers.answer: Yes, any standard T-track or clamp-on kayak carrier will work on the CURT crossbars. Just make sure you do not exceed the crossbar weight rating of 150 lbs total.

question: Do these run on the truck battery or are they solar powered?

answers.answer: They wire into your truck 12V system. Most people tap into the bed light circuit so they turn on with the factory bed light switch. They draw very little power so no battery drain concerns.

question: How bright are these for working in the truck bed at night?

answers.answer: Very bright. They run along both bed rails and illuminate the entire bed evenly. Much better than the single factory bed light. Great for camping, tailgating, or loading in the dark.

question: What kind of accessories can I attach to the Molle panels?

answers.answer: Any standard MOLLE-compatible pouches, bags, and organizers work. Many people mount first aid kits, tool pouches, recovery gear bags, and water bottle holders. The modular system is very versatile.

question: Does this mount inside the bed or on the exterior?

answers.answer: The Molle panels mount inside the bed on the side walls. They bolt to the stake pocket holes and bed rail. No drilling required. They sit flush against the bed wall.

question: Can I still haul full sheets of plywood on top of the DECKED system?

answers.answer: Yes! The DECKED platform creates a flat raised surface that can support up to 2,000 lbs on top. You can haul plywood, dirt bikes, ATVs, or anything else just like a normal bed - while keeping your tools organized underneath.

question: How much bed depth do I lose with this installed?

answers.answer: The DECKED system raises the bed floor by about 7-8 inches. You lose some vertical space but gain organized drawer storage underneath. For most cargo it is not an issue since the platform surface is still very usable.

question: Are the drawers lockable?

answers.answer: Yes, the full-size drawers have integrated locks with keys. The drawers slide out from either side of the truck. Each drawer holds up to 200 lbs and is weather-sealed.

question: Do these retract flush when not in use?

answers.answer: Yes, the BullRing anchors pop up when you need them and retract flush with the bed floor when not in use. They will not interfere with cargo sliding around or bed liners.

question: What is the working load limit per anchor?

answers.answer: Each BullRing anchor has a working load limit of 1,000 lbs. The set of four gives you 4,000 lbs of total tie-down capacity. More than enough for most cargo securing needs.

question: Does this fold inside the bed when the tailgate is up?

answers.answer: Yes, the BedXTender swings inward when you close the tailgate, creating a cargo divider inside the bed. When you lower the tailgate, it extends outward adding about 2 feet of usable bed length. Very clever design.

question: Will this work with a tonneau cover?

answers.answer: When the BedXTender is folded inward it fits under most tonneau covers. When extended outward with the tailgate down, the tonneau cover would need to be opened or removed.

question: Can I haul long items like lumber with this extended?

answers.answer: That is one of the primary use cases. The BedXTender gives you about 2 extra feet of secure bed length. The cage design keeps items from sliding off the extended tailgate. Great for lumber, pipes, and surfboards.

question: Is this different from the OverHaul HD?

answers.answer: Yes, the BedRock HD is a lighter-duty rack compared to the OverHaul HD. The BedRock is rated for 300 lbs versus 500 lbs for the OverHaul. The BedRock is more affordable and easier to install.

question: Can I use this with my BAKFlip tonneau cover?

answers.answer: The BedRock HD mounts on the bed rails above the tonneau cover line. It is compatible with most tonneau covers including the BAKFlip series. Just make sure there is clearance when folding the BAKFlip panels.

question: Can I roll this up and remove it easily for cleaning?

answers.answer: Yes, that is the main advantage of the roll-up design. You can pull it out, hose it down, and roll it back in. It is not permanently attached. Great for keeping the bed clean between dirty jobs.

question: Does this mat slide around in the bed?

answers.answer: The underside has a textured non-slip surface that grips the bed floor. It stays in place surprisingly well. The tailgate also helps hold it when closed.

question: What is this mat made of? Is it rubber?

answers.answer: It is made from WeatherTech proprietary thermoplastic material, similar to their floor mats. It is not rubber - it is lighter, more flexible, and does not have that strong rubber smell. Very durable and UV resistant.

question: What size receiver opening does this have?

answers.answer: The Class III has a standard 2-inch receiver opening. This is the most common size and fits the majority of ball mounts and hitch accessories.

question: Does the Max-Frame come with a hitch pin and clip?

answers.answer: Yes, Draw-Tite includes a standard hitch pin and clip. Many people upgrade to a locking hitch pin for security but the included one works fine.

question: Can I use this as a regular 2-inch receiver when not using the gooseneck ball?

answers.answer: Not directly, but B&W sells a Turnoverball to Receiver Hitch adapter that drops into the gooseneck hole and gives you a 2-inch receiver. Very handy for dual-purpose setups.

question: Does the ball really flip over and hide under the bed?

answers.answer: Yes, that is the defining feature. You turn the handle, the ball flips upside down into the under-bed housing, and you put a flush cover plate over the hole. Completely flat bed floor when not towing.

question: What is the towing capacity difference between Class III and Class IV?

answers.answer: Class III is rated up to 8,000 lbs gross trailer weight, while Class IV goes up to 12,000 lbs. If you tow anything over 6,000 lbs regularly, Class IV gives you more safety margin.

question: What is a front mount hitch used for?

answers.answer: Front mount hitches are used for snow plows, winch mounts, bike racks, and front-mounted cargo carriers. They are also great for hooking up to boat trailers when backing into tight spaces.

question: Will this affect my approach angle off-road?

answers.answer: The front mount hitch receiver sits slightly below the bumper line so it does reduce approach angle by a couple inches. For serious off-roading you may want to remove it or get a removable version.

question: Is Husky Towing a reputable brand?

answers.answer: Yes, Husky Towing has been making hitches for over 25 years. They are a solid mid-range option. Not as premium as B&W but well-made with good fit and finish for the price.

question: What does hidden mean - where does the receiver go?

answers.answer: The EcoHitch mounts behind the bumper fascia with just the receiver tube visible below. There is no visible cross bar or mounting hardware. It looks factory-clean from the outside.

question: Is this strong enough for a bike rack with 4 bikes?

answers.answer: Yes, the EcoHitch is rated for 300 lbs tongue weight which easily handles a 4-bike rack. The Class III version is rated for 3,500 lbs towing and 525 lbs tongue weight.

question: Is the Sportframe designed for cars or trucks?

answers.answer: The Sportframe is specifically designed for cars and small crossovers. It has a low-profile design that follows the vehicle contours. Perfect for bike racks and small cargo carriers on sedans.

question: What is the tow rating on a Class V hitch?

answers.answer: Class V hitches are rated for up to 17,000 lbs gross trailer weight with 1,700 lbs tongue weight. They use a 2.5-inch receiver opening. These are for serious commercial and heavy towing applications.

question: Is this made for Jeep Wranglers?

answers.answer: Yes, the JH46 is specifically designed for the Jeep Wrangler JK and JL models. It integrates cleanly with the Jeep frame and does not interfere with the spare tire or departure angle.

question: Does the BumpStep actually protect my bumper in parking lots?

answers.answer: Absolutely. The BumpStep extends behind the bumper and takes the impact from shopping carts and minor parking lot bumps. It also serves as a convenient step for accessing the roof. Dual purpose product.

question: Is this a combination hitch and rear bumper replacement?

answers.answer: Yes, the Ranch Hand Legend replaces your factory rear bumper entirely. It integrates a Class IV receiver hitch into a heavy-duty steel bumper. Also includes provisions for backup sensors.

question: Is this the connector that goes on the trailer or the truck?

answers.answer: The Bargman kit includes both the vehicle-side socket and the trailer-side plug. So you get the complete connection system. The socket mounts on the truck and the plug goes on the trailer.

question: Does this include the bed-mounted junction box?

answers.answer: No, you will need a separate bed-mounted junction box or connector bracket. Hopkins sells compatible ones. The harness runs from the cab to wherever you mount the connector in the bed.

question: What is the maximum amperage this converter handles?

answers.answer: The CURT powered converter handles up to 20 amps per circuit. This is enough for standard trailer lighting. For heavy-duty lighting setups with multiple LED bars, you may need a higher-rated converter.

question: What is the tongue weight rating?

answers.answer: The Blue Ox BX88302 is rated for 600 lbs tongue weight and 5,000 lbs gross towing weight. It is designed for flat towing setups rather than heavy trailer hauling.

question: Do I need to buy the spring bars separately?

answers.answer: No, the Strait-Line kit includes the head, spring bars, and all hardware. It is a complete weight distribution and sway control system in one box.

question: Is this chrome or raw steel?

answers.answer: The CURT tow ball has a chrome finish for corrosion resistance and smooth trailer coupler operation. Chrome also makes it easier to grease.

question: Does the Voyager come with the vehicle-specific wiring adapter?

answers.answer: No, the wiring adapter is sold separately. Tekonsha sells vehicle-specific adapters that make installation plug-and-play for most trucks. Look for the Tekonsha brake control wiring adapter for your vehicle.

question: Is this time-delayed or proportional?

answers.answer: The Sway Master is a time-delayed controller with sway detection. For proportional braking response, you would need to look at the Tekonsha P2 or P3 models.

question: Does the wireless remote have good range?

answers.answer: The remote works great within the cab - range is about 15-20 feet. It does not need to be line-of-sight either. Most people mount it within arm reach on the dash.

question: Will these fit with aftermarket fender flares?

answers.answer: In most cases yes, since nerf bars mount below the cab and fender flares are on the wheel wells. There should be no interference between the two.

question: How much do these weigh per side?

answers.answer: Each side weighs approximately 30 lbs. They are heavy-gauge steel so they are solid. The weight is actually an advantage - they feel very sturdy underfoot.

question: Do these require body drilling to install?

answers.answer: Most applications use existing factory holes plus 3M tape for attachment. Some models may require a few small pilot holes. Check the install instructions for your specific vehicle.

question: Are the rivet accents plastic or metal?

answers.answer: The rivet accents on the RX line are stainless steel for durability and an authentic look. They will not rust or discolor over time.

question: Does Bushwacker include the installation hardware?

answers.answer: Yes, all Bushwacker flares come with complete installation hardware including clips, screws, and 3M tape. An install guide with templates is also included.

question: Are these front and rear or rear only?

answers.answer: Bushwacker sells the Trail Armor in front and rear sets. You can buy just the rears or a complete 4-piece set depending on your needs.

question: Are Mud Skins reusable if I trade in my truck?

answers.answer: They are vehicle-specific so they will not fit a different model. They also use adhesive mounting so removal may damage them. Best to consider them permanent for the vehicle they are installed on.

question: What colors are available?

answers.answer: Rokblokz offers the Rally mud flaps in black, red, blue, yellow, and white. You can also get custom colors on special order. The logo text color can be customized too.

question: Do these fit all Mopar vehicles or just specific models?

answers.answer: Mopar splash guards are vehicle-specific. You need to order the part number that matches your exact vehicle, year, and trim level. They are designed for perfect fit on that specific application.

question: Does this come in chrome or just black?

answers.answer: Aries offers the Pro Series in both polished stainless steel and textured black powder coat. The stainless version has a chrome-like appearance that is very durable.

question: Does this guard protect the headlights?

answers.answer: The Steelcraft HD has headlight guard uprights that provide protection for the headlight area. They will deflect brush and small debris. For full headlight protection, additional guards are available.

question: Can I install these myself or is professional installation needed?

answers.answer: Headlight replacement is very doable as a DIY project. Most trucks have 3-4 bolts holding each headlight. The Spyder units are plug-and-play with the factory harness. Budget about 30 minutes per side.

question: Do these work with the factory auto-leveling system?

answers.answer: RECON projector headlights are compatible with factory auto-leveling on most applications. The mounting points match the factory headlight so the leveling motor connects properly.

question: How long do the 2Stroke LEDs actually last?

answers.answer: Morimoto rates the 2Stroke 3.0 at over 50,000 hours of use. At typical driving usage of 2-3 hours per day, that is over 40 years. They also have a 5-year warranty.

question: Will these work in my projector headlights?

answers.answer: Yes, Putco LED conversion bulbs work in both reflector and projector housings. In projector housings you may need to adjust the bulb orientation to get the best beam pattern.

question: Do these include the foam gasket seal?

answers.answer: Yes, Spyder includes a foam gasket with their tail lights to seal against the body panel. This prevents water intrusion behind the housing. Make sure to install it - do not skip this step.

question: Are these significantly more expensive than regular LED tail lights?

answers.answer: Yes, OLED technology commands a premium - typically 30-50% more than standard LED tail lights. The visual effect is noticeably different and more premium looking. Whether that is worth the extra cost is a personal decision.

question: Do the sequential signals work with the factory turn signal module?

answers.answer: Yes, the Raxiom Axial tail lights have built-in sequential circuitry. They work with the factory turn signal and hazard system with no additional modules needed.

question: Can I get these with a smoked lens?

answers.answer: ORACLE offers some models with a tinted or smoked option. Check the specific application for your vehicle. The tinted versions are still DOT compliant.

question: What beam pattern options are available?

answers.answer: Heretic offers the 6-Series in spot, flood, and combo beam patterns. For trail use, combo is the most versatile. For pure distance, go with spot.

question: Is Vision X still manufactured in the USA?

answers.answer: Vision X designs in the USA and manufactures overseas. Their quality control and warranty support remain US-based. The XPR series uses high-quality components regardless of manufacturing location.

question: Can these fog lights be aimed or adjusted after installation?

answers.answer: The G4 pods use the factory fog light adjustment mechanism if your vehicle has one. On Jeep JKs you can also shim the pods for fine-tuning the aim.

question: Is the Squadron Sport waterproof enough for mud and river crossings?

answers.answer: The Squadron Sport is IP69K rated which means it can handle high-pressure water jets and full submersion. Mud and river crossings are no problem at all for Baja Designs products.

question: Is the kit vehicle-specific or universal?

answers.answer: The Rough Country fog light kit is vehicle-specific. Each kit is designed for a particular make, model, and year range. This ensures proper fit and a factory-like appearance.

question: What wattage are the High Performance fog lights?

answers.answer: The ORACLE High Performance fogs use 20W per side. They produce significantly more light than factory fogs despite using less power thanks to LED efficiency.

question: Is the UltraGrip bed mat easy to trim for custom fit?

answers.answer: The Husky bed mat is custom molded to fit your specific truck bed, so trimming should not be necessary. If you have aftermarket accessories that require modification, the rubber material can be cut with a sharp utility knife.

question: Can I remove the DualLiner panels easily for cleaning?

answers.answer: Yes, the individual panels can be removed for cleaning. The side panels lift out and the floor mat rolls up. This is a big advantage over spray-in liners which are permanent.

question: Does BedRug XLT work in all weather?

answers.answer: Yes, the XLT material handles extreme heat and cold without issue. It will not crack in freezing temperatures or soften in summer heat. It is UV resistant and rated for year-round outdoor use.

question: Is the TechLiner custom-cut for each truck model?

answers.answer: Yes, WeatherTech laser-measures each truck bed and custom-molds the TechLiner for a precision fit. You select your exact year, make, and model when ordering.

question: Does the Penda liner prevent rust in the bed?

answers.answer: The drop-in liner covers the bed floor and walls which prevents direct moisture contact and scratches that lead to rust. However, moisture can get trapped between the liner and bed, so make sure the drain holes stay clear.

question: Does the Trifecta 2.0 fold completely flat or does it stack up?

answers.answer: When folded, the three panels stack against the cab window. The folded stack is about 12 inches tall from the bed rail. You lose some visibility in the rearview but gain full bed access.

question: Does Access offer a lifetime warranty?

answers.answer: Access offers a limited lifetime warranty on the frame and a 5-year warranty on the vinyl cover material. Their customer service is excellent if you ever need a warranty claim.

question: Does Rhino-Rack use the same T-slot accessory system as Thule?

answers.answer: Rhino-Rack uses their own accessory channel system which is not directly cross-compatible with Thule T-track. However, many universal accessories work with both. Rhino-Rack has a wide range of their own accessories.

question: Can I add accessories like awnings or lights to the CapRock?

answers.answer: Yes, the CapRock has T-track channels that accept Thule accessories including awning mounts, light brackets, and cargo nets. It is a very versatile platform.

question: What is the warranty coverage on the PowerStep?

answers.answer: AMP Research offers a 5-year / 60,000 mile warranty on the PowerStep system. This covers the motors, electrical components, and the step assemblies. Best warranty in the powered step category.

question: Is this guard compatible with a winch?

answers.answer: The Legend grille guard does not have a built-in winch mount. For winch compatibility, look at the Ranch Hand Midnight bumper which has an integrated winch tray.

question: What is the warranty on the BAKFlip MX4?

answers.answer: BAK offers a 5-year warranty on the MX4 covering material defects and craftsmanship. The matte finish and panel integrity are well-protected. They have a good reputation for honoring warranty claims.

question: Can I still use my tonneau cover with DECKED installed?

answers.answer: Yes, DECKED is compatible with most tonneau covers. The platform sits at bed rail height so covers mount on the rails above it normally. Check the DECKED compatibility list for your specific cover.

question: Does this fit a 2020 F-150?
<script>alert('xss')</script>
Does this fit a 2020 F-150?
